contents: Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc.
Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc.
Require Import Basics.Equivalences Types.Empty Types.Unit Types.Prod.

(** * Path spaces of lists *)

(** This proof was adapted from a proof given in Agda/cubical by Evan Cavallo. *)
(** We'll show that this type is equivalent to the path space [l = l'] in [list A]. *)

Fixpoint ListEq (l l' : list A) : Type :=
    match l, l' with
      | nil, nil => Unit
      | cons x xs, cons x' xs' => (x = x') * ListEq xs xs'
      | _, _ => Empty
    end.
induction l as [| a l IHl].
Local Definition encode {l1 l2} (p : l1 = l2) : ListEq l1 l2.
Local Definition decode {l1 l2} (q : ListEq l1 l2) : l1 = l2.
induction l1 as [| a l1 IHl1 ] in l2, q |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall l0 : list A, ListEq l1 l0 -> l1 = l0
forall l0 : list A, ListEq l1 l0 -> l1 = l0
forall l2 : list A, ListEq l1 l2 -> l1 = l2
forall l0 : list A, ListEq l1 l0 -> l1 = l0
forall l0 : list A, ListEq l1 l0 -> l1 = l0
forall l0 : list A, ListEq l1 l0 -> l1 = l0

contents: Local Definition decode_refl {l} : decode (reflexivity l) = idpath.

Local Definition decode_encode {l1 l2} (p : l1 = l2)
    : decode (encode p) = p.
(** By case analysis on both lists, it's easy to show that [ListEq] is [n.+1]-truncated if [A] is [n.+2]-truncated. *)

#[export] Instance istrunc_listeq n {l1 l2} {H : IsTrunc n.+2 A}
    : IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l2).

hypotheses.type: forall l0 : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l0)
forall l0 : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l0)
forall l2 : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l2)
forall l0 : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l0)
forall l0 : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (ListEq l1 l0)

contents: (** The path space of lists is a retract of [ListEq], therefore it is [n.+1]-truncated if [ListEq] is [n.+1]-truncated. By the previous result, this holds when [A] is [n.+2]-truncated. *)
#[export] Instance istrunc_list n {H : IsTrunc n.+2 A} : IsTrunc n.+2 (list A).

goals.conclusion: forall x y : list A, IsTrunc n.+1 (x = y)

contents: (** With a little more work, we can show that [ListEq] is also a retract of the path space. *)

Local Definition encode_decode {l1 l2} (p : ListEq l1 l2)
    : encode (decode p) = p.

hypotheses.type: forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l2 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l2), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l2 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l2), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0

goals.conclusion: snd (encode (ap011 cons r (decode p))) = encode (decode p)

hypotheses.type: forall (l0 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l0), encode (decode p0) = p0
forall (l2 : list A) (p0 : ListEq l1 l2), encode (decode p0) = p0

contents: (** Giving us a way of characterizing paths in lists. *)
